Event: David Fairchild born in Lansing, Michigan
Fairchild begins work at USDA as assistant in plant pathology section
Fairchild departs for Europe on SS Fulda; meets Barbour Lathrop aboard ship
Fairchild arrives in Naples; Lathrop offers $1000/year to be his travel companion; studies at Naples Zoological Station
Dec 17 - Fairchild's first plant introduction: collects citron cuttings from Corsica for USDA
Fairchild travels to Java to study termites; visits Buitenzorg botanical garden (now Bogor)
Fairchild and Lathrop visit Sumatra together
Fairchild visits Australia during Pacific tour with Lathrop, notes eucalyptus
Fairchild visits Fiji with Lathrop; encounters kava, learns about former cannibalism practices
Fairchild returns to Washington; co-founds Office of Seed and Plant Introduction with Walter Swingle
Fairchild and Lathrop begin second world tour; collect chayote and mangoes in Jamaica
Fairchild collects mango varieties in Trinidad
Major find: Fairchild discovers avocados in Chile; describes them as the veritable fruit of paradise
Fairchild and Lathrop cross the Andes; collect bella sombra tree in Argentina
Fairchild collects Itamaraca mango in Brazil
Fairchild collects fava beans during London visit
Fairchild discovers broccoli in Venice
Fairchild collects sultanina (Thompson) seedless grapes at Botanical Garden of Padua
Oct 31 - Fairchild arrives in Egypt; collects Jannovitch Egyptian cotton, sesame, chickpeas, dates, and many other crops
Jan - 40-day cruise through Malay Islands with Lathrop; collects pomelo from island of Sekar (Ceram)
Fairchild collects carabao mango in Philippines, later known as champagne mango
Fairchild collects rice varieties, water chestnuts, and guava in Canton
Dec 18 - Bohemian Sems hops arrive on American soil; collected by Fairchild for American brewers
Fairchild collects grape varieties, carob, and hazelnuts in Italy
Fairchild introduces kale from Austria-Hungary to the United States
Feb - Fairchild collects pistachios, seedless lemons from island of Poros, and lentils in Greece
Fairchild collects Alphonso mango and approximately 80 other mango varieties in Bombay
Fairchild collects mangosteen in Saigon;  favorite fruit
Fairchild travels to Baghdad; collects date palm offshoots, wheat, barley, and chickpeas
Fairchild collects nectarines in Quetta (then British India)

Crop: Wasabi; loquat; zoysia grass; persimmon; flowering cherry trees; udo
Event: Fairchild visits Japan; collects wasabi, loquat, zoysia grass, persimmon, udo, and first encounters flowering cherry trees

Crop: Kaffir orange; carissa plum; miniature pineapple
Event: Fairchild and Lathrop circumnavigate Africa; collect Kaffir orange, carissa plum, and mini pineapple - their last expedition together
Fairchild marries Marian Bell, daughter of Alexander Graham Bell
Frank Meyer hired by Fairchild after interview at USDA office
July 27 - Frank Meyer departs for first China expedition (3 years); first assignment: find grindstone persimmon in Beijing
Meyer discovers the Meyer lemon near Beijing - a mix between a mandarin and a citron, sweeter and tarter; takes cutting with bowie knife
Son Alexander Graham Bell Fairchild born; cherry trees bloom at Fairchild home in Chevy Chase

Crop: Oats; millet; watermelon; cotton; wild pears; persimmons; Manchurian spinach; asparagus; soybeans; bamboo (30 types); red blackberries; wild apricots; zelkova trees; Chinese holly; white-barked pines; lilacs (18 forms); viburnum; spirea; rhododendron; daphne; lilies; lawn sedge
Event: Meyer first expedition: walks 20+ miles/day across China, Manchuria, Korea, and eastern Siberia over 3 years. Sends first soybeans and asparagus to officially enter the US. Returns July 7 1908 with 20 tons of plant material via Standard Oil steamer
California Governor Hiram Johnson creates unilateral Quarantine Division; prohibits all fruit from Hawaii

Country: China; Caucasus; Russian Turkestan; Chinese Turkestan; Siberia
Crop: Wild apricots; asparagus; plums; cherries; soybeans
Event: Meyer second expedition: sends 30 tons of Asiatic plants to San Francisco. All seized fumigated and burned by California quarantine officials

Crop: Brazilian cherries; cashew wine; guava jelly; jaboticaba
Event: Oct 4 - Wilson Popenoe departs on SS Vandyck (with Theodore Roosevelt aboard) for Brazil; searches for better navel orange but confirms existing variety is superior

Crop: Walnuts; hazelnuts; chestnuts (blight-resistant); jujubes; pears; apples; peaches; haw
Event: Meyer third expedition: discovers Chinese chestnut trees resistant to blight devastating American chestnuts. Sets record for plant material shipped from China. All passes quarantine
Wilson Popenoe spends 3 years collecting avocados in Guatemala on horseback living on beans and chayotes. Returns with 23 best varieties in tin tubes
Fairchilds buy property in Coconut Grove on Biscayne Bay; Lathrop names it the Kampong (Malay for compound)
Meyer fourth and final expedition: deteriorating mental health. Near Hsing-shan-hsien finds ginkgo biloba with edible seeds. Collects Ichang lemons. Sends final shipments
Quarantine #37 enacted: ends all private plant importations. Plant Immigrant Bulletin terminated. Fairchild program greatly diminished
First Frank Meyer Medal for plant introduction awarded to Barbour Lathrop
David Fairchild dies at the Kampong under a Javanese ficus tree in August at age 84
